title: Log Analytics retention shorter than incident timeline
narrative: Log Analytics workspace retention is 30 days; the industry average incident dwell time is 180+ days. By the time an intrusion is detected, the logs covering the initial compromise are already rotated out.

steps.action: Maintain quiet presence for 90 days before noisy activity.
steps.gain: Initial compromise data aged out of workspace.

steps.action: Cannot determine patient zero or initial vector.

services_at_risk: Every system relying on this workspace for IR

title: Diagnostic settings missing on Key Vault
narrative: A Key Vault has no diagnostic settings streaming to a SIEM. AccessPolicyChange and SecretGet events are invisible to defenders. An attacker with vault read can enumerate every secret and the security team never sees it.

steps.action: List and read every secret; each GetSecret logs only in vault itself.
Clean up vault-local logs (if retention config allows).

title: Activity Log alerts missing on IAM writes
narrative: No Activity Log alert is configured for Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write. When an attacker grants themselves Owner on a resource group, SOC has no real-time alert — the change lives in the log but nobody's watching.

steps.action: Grant self Owner via role assignment.
Operate freely; evidence lives in log but never alerts.

title: Defender for Cloud disabled + no Sentinel
narrative: Defender for Cloud is at Free tier and there's no Sentinel workspace. There is no behavioral detection, no anomaly scoring, no threat-intel correlation. Every other control has to work perfectly because there is no second layer.

steps.action: Operate with no behavioral analytics running.
Long dwell time; no mean-time-to-detect signal.

title: Sentinel analytics rules disabled for cost
narrative: Sentinel is deployed but the high-fidelity analytics rules (brute force, impossible travel, new admin) are disabled to reduce cost. The workspace holds the data but produces no alerts — a tool without eyes.

steps.action: Disable detection rules to drop ingest.
Known TTPs pass unnoticed.

title: Azure Monitor action groups inactive
narrative: Alerts are configured but the action groups point to mailboxes nobody reads or webhooks to decommissioned systems. The alert fires; the response never comes.

steps.action: Fire into dead mailbox / broken webhook.
Continue operating; alert triggered but unseen.

title: Application Insights sample rate too aggressive
narrative: App Insights sampling is set to 5%. Only 1 in 20 requests is logged. During an incident, the SOC cannot tell whether an anomalous request pattern is real or a sampling artefact. Sparse data forces conservative (slow) decisions.

steps.action: Most attacker requests never land in App Insights.
Cannot distinguish real attack from baseline noise.

title: Subscription without Activity Log export
narrative: The subscription's Activity Log is kept at default 90-day retention (in Azure) with no export to Log Analytics or Storage. At day 91, every control-plane action from day 1 is gone — deleted resource-group audit trail, role-assignment history, everything.

steps.action: Day 91: oldest Activity Log entries purge.
Cannot reconstruct long-ago privileged operations.

title: Critical resources without resource locks
narrative: Production storage accounts, key vaults, and databases have no delete locks. A compromised Contributor or an accidental 'rm -rf' via Terraform can destroy them in seconds — with no two-step confirmation.

steps.action: Terraform destroy or Portal delete on unlocked resource.
Extended outage; potential data loss depending on backup state.

title: Secure Score low + no remediation tracking
narrative: Defender Secure Score is below 40% for months with no remediation trend. The control plane knows what's broken but nobody's assigned to fix it. Every vulnerability the score reflects remains live.

steps.action: Recommendations accumulate; score stays flat.
Exploit any of the documented gaps.
